rationale: The cited paper appears only in grouped literature-review citations, mainly to support broad claims that consumers treat time and money differently and may prefer time spending for hedonic goods. The citing paper does not engage Okada (2005)'s specific justification mechanism, methods, or findings in its own theorizing, design, or interpretation beyond this background support. Because the citation functions as general prior evidence rather than a distinct building block for this paper's core attribution-based account, the role is best classified as a passing/background mention.
